explicit_text: I was born in the United Kingdom in 1998, where my parents had settled after graduating from university in Cuba. My brother was also born there three years later. We lived in the UK during a time when crime rates were rising, which influenced my parents' decision to move us to Canada. My father stayed behind in the UK for a few years to work before joining us in Canada. The UK was our home for the first several years of my life, but eventually we left it for a safer environment.

We lived there during a time when crime rates were rising, which influenced my parents' decision to move us to Canada. My father stayed behind in that country for a few years to work before joining us in Canada. That place was our home for the first several years of my life, but eventually we left it for a safer environment.

explicit_text: My parents are originally from Ghana, and that's where our extended family, including grandparents, aunts, uncles, and cousins, live. Although we moved to the UK and later to Canada, Ghana has always been the core of our family roots. Moving to Canada meant that visits to Ghana became much less frequent due to longer and more expensive flights. Despite the distance, Ghana remains an important part of our identity and family connections. I grew up knowing that my heritage is deeply tied to Ghana.

explicit_text: My parents met at university in Cuba back in 1988, which is where their story together began. They both graduated there before moving to the United Kingdom to start their family. Cuba played a significant role in shaping their early adult lives and careers. It was the place where they connected and laid the foundation for our family. Even though I was born much later in the UK, Cuba remains an important part of my family's history.

explicit_text: In 2005, when I was seven, my mother, brother, and I moved to Canada from the UK, seeking a safer and friendlier environment. We first visited Guelph, Ontario in 2003, where some of my parents' university friends lived, and that visit convinced my parents Canada was the right place to raise us. My father joined us a few years later after securing a job here. Growing up in Canada gave my brother and me a good education and a multicultural environment where differences are celebrated. Since arriving, we've moved around Canada a few times and embraced all the new experiences it has offered.

explicit_text: We first visited Guelph, Ontario in 2003 during a vacation, where some of my parents' close university friends lived. My parents loved how safe and friendly Guelph felt compared to the UK, especially given the rising crime rates there at the time. This visit was pivotal because it convinced them that Guelph was the right place to raise me and my brother. It was the starting point of our family's immigration journey to Canada. Guelph holds a special place in our story as the city that inspired our move.
